Item.name: Max
Item.description: Highest value in the input waveform

Item.name: Min
Item.description: Lowest value in the input waveform

Item.name: Pk2Pk
Item.description: Difference between the maximum and minimum data values

Item.name: Top
Item.description: The most frequent level above the middle position of the waveform is considered the top level. If the most frequent level is not correctly defined, the top level is treated as the same as the maximum value.

Item.name: Base
Item.description: The most frequent level below the middle position of the waveform is considered the base level. If the most frequent level is not correctly defined, the base level is treated as the same as the minimum value.

Item.name: Mid
Item.description: The level at the middle position between the top level and the base level of the waveform.

Item.name: Amplitude
Item.description: The difference between the top level and the base level of the waveform.

Item.name: Average
Item.description: average value of data

Item.name: RMS
Item.description: Root mean square of measured data

Item.name: Stddev
Item.description: Standard deviation of data

Item.name: POverShoot
Item.description: Overshoot after rising edge: (maximum value at rising edge - top value)/(top value - base value)

Item.name: NOverShoot
Item.description: Overshoot after falling edge: (base value - minimum value at falling edge) / (top value - base value)

Item.name: Area
Item.description: Area of the waveform

Item.name: Period
Item.description: The time interval required for the signal to repeat itself on the time axis. It is generally represented by the time difference between two adjacent waveforms crossing the reference middle level (RM) in the same direction. RM is the 50% amplitude position.

Item.name: Freq
Item.description: The number of repetitions per second of a periodic signal, which is a physical quantity and is the reciprocal of the period. It is generally represented by the reciprocal of the time difference between two adjacent waveforms crossing the reference middle level (RM) in the same direction. RM is the 50% amplitude position.

Item.name: Rise
Item.description: The time required for the edge to rise from the reference base level (RB), cross the reference middle level (RM), and reach the reference top level (RT). RB is the 10% amplitude position, RM is the 50% amplitude position, and RT is the 90% amplitude position.

Item.name: Fall
Item.description: The time required for the edge to fall from the reference top level (RT), cross the reference middle level (RM), and reach the reference base level (RB). RB is the 10% amplitude position, RM is the 50% amplitude position, and RT is the 90% amplitude position.

Item.name: PWidth
Item.description: The positive pulse width is the time from when the rise edge's reference middle level (RM), crosses the reference top level (RT), and reaches the next fall edge's reference middle level (RM). RM is the 50% amplitude position, and RT is the 90% amplitude position.

Item.name: NWidth
Item.description: The negative pulse width is the time from when the fall edge's reference middle level (RM)，crosses the reference yop level (RB), and reaches the next rise edge's reference middle level (RM). RB is the 10% amplitude position, and RM is the 50% amplitude position.

Item.name: Duty
Item.description: The ratio of the waveform’s positive pulse width to the period, expressed as a percentage.

Item.name: NDuty
Item.description: The ratio of the waveform’s negative pulse width to the period, expressed as a percentage.

Item.name: T@max
Item.description: The point corresponding to the first maximum

Item.name: T@min
Item.description: The point corresponding to the first minimum

Item.name: CycMax
Item.description: Maximum value of the waveform in one cycle

Item.name: CycMin
Item.description: Minimum value of the waveform in one cycle

Item.name: CycRMS
Item.description: RMS value of a waveform over one cycle

Item.name: CycAverage
Item.description: The average value of the waveform over one cycle

Item.name: CycPeak
Item.description: Peak-to-peak value of the waveform in one cycle

Item.name: CycArea
Item.description: Area of the waveform over one cycle

Item.name: CycMid
Item.description: Median value of the waveform in one cycle

Item.name: Rise@lv
Item.description: The time required for the edge to rise from the reference base level (RB), cross the reference middle level (RM), and reach the reference top level (RT).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Fall@lv
Item.description: The time required for the edge to fall from the reference top level (RT), cross the reference middle level (RM), and reach the reference base level (RB).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Period@lv
Item.description: The time interval required for the signal to repeat itself on the time axis. It is generally represented by the time difference between two adjacent waveforms crossing the reference middle level (RM) in the same direction.RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Freq@lv
Item.description: The number of repetitions per second of a periodic signal, which is a physical quantity and is the reciprocal of the period. It is generally represented by the reciprocal of the time difference between two adjacent waveforms crossing the reference middle level (RM) in the same direction.RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Width@lv
Item.description: The pulse width is the time from when the edge's reference middle level (RM), crosses the reference end level (RT or RB), and reaches the next edge's reference middle level (RM).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Duty@lv
Item.description: The ratio of the waveform’s pulse width to the period, expressed as a percentage.RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Phase@lv
Item.description: The phase difference between two waveforms in themselves' periods (360 °) when the first rise edge passes through the reference middle level.RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: RRDelay@lv
Item.description: The time difference between the rise edge of the first waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM) and the rise edg of the second waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: FFDelay@lv
Item.description: The time difference between the fall edge of the first waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM) and the fall edge of the second waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: RFDelay@lv
Item.description: The time difference between the rise edge of the first waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM) and the fall edge of the second waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: FRDelay@lv
Item.description: The time difference between the rise edge of the first waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM) and the rise edge of the second waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM).RB, RM, and RT can be specified in the threshold setting function.

Item.name: Skew
Item.description: The time difference between the rise edge of the first waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM) and the rise edge of the second waveform passing through the reference middle level (RM).RM is the 50% amplitude position.

Item.name: WfmLength
Item.description: Number of sample points of the waveform data involved in the measurement

Item.name: DataRate
Item.description: The inverse of the unit interval

Item.name: UI
Item.description: Time difference between two consecutive bits

Item.name: RSlewRate
Item.description: Amplitude changing rate of the rising edge jumping from the lowest reference level to the highest reference level

Item.name: FSlewRate
Item.description: Amplitude changing rate of the falling edge jumping from the highest reference level to the lowest reference level

Item.name: Cycles
Item.description: The number of cycles in a periodic waveform

Item.name: RiseEdges
Item.description: The number of raise edge in a periodic waveform.

Item.name: FallEdges
Item.description: The number of fall edge in a periodic waveform.

Item.name: PosPulses
Item.description: The number of positive pulse in a periodic waveform.

Item.name: NegPulses
Item.description: The number of negative pulse in a periodic waveform.

Item.name: BurstLen
Item.description: 50% of the first edge to 50% of the last edge

Item.name: BurstCycle
Item.description: Calculate the period when burst width and burst interval are satisfied

Item.name: BurstInterval
Item.description: Calculate the interval between two burst events

Item.name: BurstPeriod
Item.description: Calculate the burst period when the burst width and burst interval are satisfied

Item.name: NPeriods
Item.description: Time required to complete multiple cycles

Item.name: OutsideTime
Item.description: The time the signal level remains outside the high and low reference level range

Item.name: HighTime
Item.description: The time the signal level remains greater than the high reference level

Item.name: LowTime
Item.description: The time the signal level remains less than the low reference level

Item.name: SetupTime
Item.description: The time from when the specified intermediate reference level on the data source was exceeded to when the specified intermediate reference level on the clock source was last exceeded

Item.name: HoldTime
Item.description: The time from when the specified intermediate reference level on the clock source was exceeded to when the specified intermediate reference level on the data source was last exceeded

Item.name: PPreShoot
Item.description: Preshoot before rising edge: (base value - minimum value at rising edge)/(top value - base value)

Item.name: NPreShoot
Item.description: Preshoot before falling edge: (maximum value at falling edge - top value)/(top value - base value)

Item.name: HistMu1Sigma
Item.description: Mean + standard deviation range of histogram samples

Item.name: HistMu2Sigma
Item.description: Mean + 2*standard deviation range of histogram samples

Item.name: HistMu3Sigma
Item.description: Mean + 3*standard deviation range of histogram samples

Item.name: HistMode
Item.description: Location of the highest peak in the histogram

Item.name: HistMean
Item.description: Sample mean in the histogram

Item.name: HistSigma
Item.description: Sample standard deviation in the histogram

Item.name: HistMax
Item.description: The maximum sample value in the histogram

Item.name: HistMin
Item.description: The minimum sample value in the histogram

Item.name: HistMid
Item.description: Sample median in the histogram

Item.name: HistRange
Item.description: Sample peak-to-peak value in the histogram

Item.name: HistMaxPop
Item.description: Number of peak points in the histogram

Item.name: HistTotalPop
Item.description: Total number of samples in the histogram

Item.name: HistWfmCnt
Item.description: Total number of waveforms in the histogram
